question: Will Luke Bronin be the Democratic nominee for CT-01?
description:
This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Democratic Party to contest the CT-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Democratic primary will take place on August 11, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democrat sources, including https://democrats.org/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

events.title: CT-01 Democratic Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Democratic Party to contest the CT-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Democratic primary will take place on August 11, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democrat sources, including https://democrats.org/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment in the CT-01 Democratic primary heavily favors Luke Bronin at 46.5%, driven by a recent Emerson College poll showing him edging incumbent John Larson 36%-32% among likely voters, with 17% undecided ahead of the August 13 contest. Bronin's momentum stems from strong Q2 fundraising—over $450,000 raised versus Larson's lower totals—and his record as Hartford mayor appealing to moderates seeking change from Larson's 25-year tenure amid progressive critiques. Larson retains union and party establishment backing, holding steady at 33.5%, while lower odds for Ruth Fortune (6%), Jillian Gilchrest (4.5%), and Mark Stewart Greenstein (2.1%) reflect their limited poll support and resources, underscoring poll-driven uncertainty in this tight race.

question: Will Buffalo Bills win the 2026 AFC East?
description:
This market will resolve according to the team that is declared as the AFC East division champion for the 2026 NFL regular season.

If multiple teams tie atop the division, this market will resolve according to the official tiebreak procedure of the NFL.

If the 2026 NFL regular season is cancelled, postponed after January 17, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared for this division within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the NFL; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Pro Football: AFC East Champion
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the team that is declared as the AFC East division champion for the 2026 NFL regular season.

If multiple teams tie atop the division, this market will resolve according to the official tiebreak procedure of the NFL.

If the 2026 NFL regular season is cancelled, postponed after January 17, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared for this division within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the NFL; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Buffalo Bills lead trader consensus at 54% implied probability for the AFC East crown, anchored by Josh Allen's elite quarterbacking and three consecutive division titles despite trading Stefon Diggs, with their defense bolstered by Ed Oliver and Matt Milano's return from injury. New England Patriots have surged to 34.5% on optimism around Jerod Mayo's head coaching debut, Drake Maye's No. 3 draft pick, and key free-agent additions like Michael Onwenu amid a rebuilt roster. Miami Dolphins sit at 8.5% following Tua Tagovailoa's $212 million extension but tempered by injury histories for Jaylen Waddle and Jalen Ramsey, while New York Jets lag at 8% due to Aaron Rodgers' Achilles recovery and offensive line concerns, with training camp reports showing no major setbacks shifting sentiment further.

eventMetadata.context_description: Baltimore Ravens lead trader consensus at 46.5% implied probability to win the AFC North, driven by their division-best 5-2 record, elite defense allowing just 16.7 points per game, and Lamar Jackson's MVP-caliber play amid a three-game win streak. Pittsburgh Steelers (27%) have surged on a four-game winning streak with Russell Wilson's efficient debut starts replacing struggling Kenny Pickett, bolstering their 4-2 mark and top-ranked scoring defense. Cincinnati Bengals (24.5%) hold upside via Joe Burrow's return from injury and a recent 41-38 overtime upset over Baltimore, despite a middling 3-4 start. Cleveland Browns (10%) lag at 2-5 with Deshaun Watson's season-ending Achilles tear crippling their offense.

eventMetadata.context_description: Texans' surge to 6-2 atop the AFC South standings, capped by a gritty Monday night win over the Colts, anchors trader consensus but leaves room for challengers amid lingering uncertainties. Jacksonville's 34.5% implied probability reflects optimism around Trevor Lawrence's return despite his four-interception stinker against Cleveland, paired with the league's easiest remaining schedule and home-heavy slate. Houston's edge is checked by DE Will Anderson's season-ending injury and a brutal stretch facing AFC North foes, while the Colts' 4-4 mark hinges on Anthony Richardson's recovery and Shane Steichen's play-calling. Titans lag but boast divisional head-to-heads that could spark chaos, underscoring the division's parity and volatility with 10 weeks left.

Los Angeles Chargers (28.5%) ride Justin Herbert's arm and Jim Harbaugh's grit but stumble with offensive line woes. Raiders lag at 11% amid 2-10 woes and QB instability, leaving the top trio's divisional clashes—Broncos-Chargers finale, Chiefs' sweeps—to decide this bunched race.

question: Will Kansas City Chiefs win the 2026 AFC West?
description:
This market will resolve according to the team that is declared as the AFC West division champion for the 2026 NFL regular season.

If multiple teams tie atop the division, this market will resolve according to the official tiebreak procedure of the NFL.

If the 2026 NFL regular season is cancelled, postponed after January 17, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared for this division within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the NFL; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Another 7.0 or above earthquake by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if one or more earthquakes with a magnitude of 7.0 or higher occur anywhere on Earth between market creation and the listed date ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market is the United States Geological Survey (USGS) Earthquake Hazards Program (https://earthquake.usgs.gov/earthquakes/browse/significant.php#sigdef).

If an earthquake of substantial size has occurred within this market's timeframe but not yet appeared on the resolution source, this market may remain open until the end of the month following resolution time or until the earthquake in question otherwise appears on the resolution source. If such an earthquake has not appeared on the resolution source by that date, another credible resolution source will be used. 

After a qualifying earthquake is registered, this market will remain open for 24 hours to account for any revisions to its recorded magnitude. After 24 hours, this market will resolve according to the latest provided data.

events.title: Another 7.0 or above earthquake by...?

If an earthquake of substantial size has occurred within this market's timeframe but not yet appeared on the resolution source, this market may remain open until the end of the month following resolution time or until the earthquake in question otherwise appears on the resolution source. If such an earthquake has not appeared on the resolution source by that date, another credible resolution source will be used. 

After a qualifying earthquake is registered, this market will remain open for 24 hours to account for any revisions to its recorded magnitude. After 24 hours, this market will resolve according to the latest provided data.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for another magnitude 7.0 or higher earthquake leans toward moderate implied probabilities, driven primarily by USGS baseline data showing 12-20 such events annually worldwide on average, following a Poisson-like distribution of seismic activity along plate boundaries. The last confirmed M7+ was a 7.2 off the Philippines on August 30, with no major aftershocks escalating since, keeping recent global counts near historical norms for 2024 at around 11 events year-to-date. Authoritative USGS monitoring via the National Earthquake Information Center underscores inherent unpredictability—no reliable short-term forecasts exist—but heightened vigilance persists in the Pacific Ring of Fire amid ongoing swarm activity in Japan and Indonesia. Traders should watch daily USGS updates for real-time catalogs, as any uptick could shift market odds before resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Philadelphia Eagles lead trader consensus at 39% implied probability for the NFC East crown, bolstered by their NFC-best 10-2 record, elite defense allowing the fewest points league-wide, and a favorable remaining schedule featuring winnable divisional clashes. Dallas Cowboys hold 30% despite slipping to 6-6 after Dak Prescott's season-ending hamstring injury, with backup Mike Locke's inconsistency tempering optimism amid a tough road slate. Washington Commanders' 15.5% reflects rookie QB Jayden Daniels' six-game win streak and 7-5 mark, injecting upset potential into a congested wild-card hunt. New York Giants lag at 14.5% and 2-10, hampered by QB instability post-Daniel Jones benching and porous defense. Recent Eagles' comeback win over Baltimore and Cowboys' home loss to Giants have widened the gap in division futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: The NFC North remains a nail-biter with Green Bay Packers holding a slim edge in trader consensus at 33.5% implied probability, just ahead of Detroit Lions at 30.5%, reflecting their near-even records through nine weeks—Lions 7-1 atop the standings, Packers and Minnesota Vikings at 5-3, Chicago Bears lurking at 4-4. Packers' recent momentum from Jordan Love's efficient play (68% completion, 9 TDs last five games) and a softer late schedule bolsters their case, while Lions' dominance (top-ranked scoring offense) is tempered by Aidan Hutchinson's season-ending injury and a brutal remaining slate against playoff contenders. Vikings' Sam Darnold surge (2,100+ yards, 17 TDs) keeps them viable amid Justin Jefferson's return, and Bears' Caleb Williams growth adds upset potential, underscoring the division's parity where one skid could flip the script.

question: Will March 2026 be the 1st hottest on record?
description:
This market will resolve based on the data for the Global Land-Ocean Temperature Index for March 2026 versus the data points available for all other Marches on record. 

Note: If March 2026 is tied for first, second, or third hottest with another year, it will qualify for the bracket it ties with.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the figures found in the table titled "GLOBAL Land-Ocean Temperature Index in 0.01 degrees Celsius" under the column "Mar" (https://data.giss.nasa.gov/gistemp/tabledata_v4/GLB.Ts+dSST.txt). If NASA's "Global Temperature Index" is rendered permanently unavailable, other information from NASA may be used.
If no information for March 2026 is provided by NASA by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, a consensus of credible sources will be used to resolve this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors March 2026 ranking 4th or lower on the global temperature record (97.8% implied probability), driven by NOAA and Copernicus data showing March 2024 as the hottest ever at +0.68°C above the 1991-2020 baseline, followed by 2016 (+0.57°C) and 2023. Ongoing global warming is offset by forecasts of persistent La Niña conditions through early 2026 (71% probability per NOAA), which typically suppress sea surface temperatures and global anomalies by 0.1-0.2°C compared to El Niño peaks. This positions 2026 below recent records amid cyclical ENSO cooling. Realistic challenges include an abrupt El Niño resurgence or amplified marine heatwaves exceeding model projections.

question: Will March 2026 be the 2nd hottest on record?
description:
This market will resolve based on the data for the Global Land-Ocean Temperature Index for March 2026 versus the data points available for all other Marches on record. 

Note: If March 2026 is tied for first, second, or third hottest with another year, it will qualify for the bracket it ties with.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the figures found in the table titled "GLOBAL Land-Ocean Temperature Index in 0.01 degrees Celsius" under the column "Mar" (https://data.giss.nasa.gov/gistemp/tabledata_v4/GLB.Ts+dSST.txt). If NASA's "Global Temperature Index" is rendered permanently unavailable, other information from NASA may be used.
If no information for March 2026 is provided by NASA by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, a consensus of credible sources will be used to resolve this market.

question: Will fewer than 70 tornadoes occur in the United States in March 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of tornadoes recorded in the United States during the specified month, based on the monthly count published on the National Centers for Environmental Information U.S. Tornadoes Time Series page (see: https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/access/monitoring/tornadoes/time-series).

Only tornadoes appearing in the final NCEI dataset for that month will count.

As of market creation, the relevant report is scheduled to be released on April 8, 2026, at 5:01 PM GMT+1 or 11:01 AM ET (Release schedule: https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/access/monitoring/dyk/monthly-releases). The market will resolve based on the first relevant tornado count published on the NCEI tornado time-series page after this scheduled release time.

If the value published after this scheduled release time is labeled preliminary, it will still determine resolution, and the market will resolve independently of any subsequent revisions, corrections, or retroactive adjustments. 

The market will not resolve based on any preliminary values published before the scheduled release time.

If no data is published by the scheduled release time, or if the NCEI website is temporarily unavailable, this market will remain open until that data is made available. If the relevant data is not made available by the date of the next scheduled publication ET, this market will resolve based on available data for the most recent prior month. If the NCEI website becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve using another credible source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders' near-unanimous consensus on 150+ US tornadoes in March stems from the Storm Prediction Center's (SPC) preliminary count surpassing 200 confirmed twisters as of early April, shattering the typical monthly average of around 80. This surge was fueled by repeated high-risk outbreaks, including supercell-favorable setups on March 14-17 and 24-27 across the Plains and Southeast, driven by persistent wind shear and CAPE exceeding 2000 J/kg per NOAA soundings. Historical data shows March 2024 as the second-most active on record since 1950. A challenge would require unprecedented downgrading of reports to non-tornadic damage—unlikely given enhanced radar networks and ground surveys—but a quiet final week minimized further upside.

question: Will Mexico’s February unemployment rate be less than or equal to 2.4%?
description:
This market will resolve according to the unemployment rate (15 years and over, total) reported by the Mexican National Institute of Statistics and Geography (INEGI) for February 2026.

The resolution source for this market is the National Survey of Occupation and Employment, published by INEGI every month at https://en.www.inegi.org.mx/programas/enoe/15ymas/

The next data release is scheduled for March 27, 2026. This market will resolve as soon as the relevant data is issued. Any revisions to the data after the first release will not count toward this market's resolution.

If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

Note: the resolution source for this market reports unemployment to one decimal point. Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

events.title: February Unemployment Rate - Mexico
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the unemployment rate (15 years and over, total) reported by the Mexican National Institute of Statistics and Geography (INEGI) for February 2026.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Mexico's February unemployment rate tilts strongly toward ≤2.4% at 67% implied probability, driven by robust nearshoring inflows and manufacturing expansion that bolstered January's 2.6% rate from INEGI. Consensus economist forecasts hover around 2.5%, yet Polymarket odds reflect trader optimism from surging formal job creation—over 250,000 added last quarter—and cooling inflation enabling Banxico's steady 11.25% policy rate. Secondary bets on 2.8% (19%) and 2.5% (11%) account for potential seasonal services sector softness, with INEGI's March 22 release as the key resolution trigger. Historical lows near 2.4% in 2023 reinforce this market-implied downside bias amid resilient GDP growth.

events.title: February Unemployment Rate - Mexico
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the unemployment rate (15 years and over, total) reported by the Mexican National Institute of Statistics and Geography (INEGI) for February 2026.

The resolution source for this market is the National Survey of Occupation and Employment, published by INEGI every month at https://en.www.inegi.org.mx/programas/enoe/15ymas/

The next data release is scheduled for March 27, 2026. This market will resolve as soon as the relevant data is issued. Any revisions to the data after the first release will not count toward this market's resolution.

If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

Note: the resolution source for this market reports unemployment to one decimal point. Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Mexico's February unemployment rate tilts strongly toward ≤2.4% at 67% implied probability, driven by robust nearshoring inflows and manufacturing expansion that bolstered January's 2.6% rate from INEGI. Consensus economist forecasts hover around 2.5%, yet Polymarket odds reflect trader optimism from surging formal job creation—over 250,000 added last quarter—and cooling inflation enabling Banxico's steady 11.25% policy rate. Secondary bets on 2.8% (19%) and 2.5% (11%) account for potential seasonal services sector softness, with INEGI's March 22 release as the key resolution trigger. Historical lows near 2.4% in 2023 reinforce this market-implied downside bias amid resilient GDP growth.

question: Will Atlanta Falcons win the 2026 NFC South?
description:
This market will resolve according to the team that is declared as the NFC South division champion for the 2026 NFL regular season.

If multiple teams tie atop the division, this market will resolve according to the official tiebreak procedure of the NFL.

If the 2026 NFL regular season is cancelled, postponed after January 17, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared for this division within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the NFL; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Pro Football: NFC South Champion
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the team that is declared as the NFC South division champion for the 2026 NFL regular season.

If multiple teams tie atop the division, this market will resolve according to the official tiebreak procedure of the NFL.

If the 2026 NFL regular season is cancelled, postponed after January 17, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared for this division within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the NFL; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The NFC South's championship race stays razor-tight due to parity across all four teams, with Tampa Bay Buccaneers (32%) and Carolina Panthers (31%) edging implied probabilities amid recent surges, while Saints (26%) and Falcons (23.5%) lurk close. Buccaneers hold a slim lead atop standings at 6-5 after Baker Mayfield's four-TD outburst in a Thursday rout of Giants, bolstering their 4-1 division mark. Panthers (4-6) gained traction with Bryce Young's efficient play and wins over Giants/NFL's worst defenses, fueling comeback talk despite turnover woes. Saints (5-5) rely on Alvin Kamara's grind but falter sans Derek Carr fully healthy; Falcons (6-4) boast Kirk Cousins' arm yet leak points defensively. Gauntlet schedules ahead, including rivalry clashes, keep trader consensus bunched near even money.

eventMetadata.context_description: The NFC West division lead hinges on a nail-biting duel between the Seattle Seahawks and Los Angeles Rams, both holding near-identical records after Seattle's recent 20-17 upset victory over the Rams on Thursday Night Football, flipping momentum. Seahawks' defense has surged with five takeaways in that win, bolstering their edge in total defense ranking, while Rams quarterback Matthew Stafford's return to full health has fueled a four-game win streak prior. San Francisco 49ers have faded to third amid key injuries like Christian McCaffrey's season-ending knee issue and a porous run defense, dropping their playoff odds. Arizona Cardinals trail as longshots despite Kyler Murray's flashes. Remaining schedules favor Seattle and L.A. with softer opponents, sustaining the top-two trader consensus at 38.5% and 35.0%.

question: Will the Bank of Korea decrease the base rate after the May Meeting?
description:
This market will resolve according to the change in the base rate resulting from the Bank of Korea’s May monetary policy meeting, relative to the level it was prior to this meeting.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the Bank of Korea after its May 28, 2026 policy-setting meeting, as listed on the official Bank of Korea meeting schedule: https://www.bok.or.kr/eng/bbs/E0000627/view.do?nttId=10094301&searchCnd=1&searchKwd=&depth2=400417&depth3=400022&depth=400022&pageUnit=10&pageIndex=1&programType=newsDataEng&menuNo=400022&oldMenuNo=400022

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Korea's policy statement for their May 28, 2026 meeting with relevant data is issued. If no decision on the base rate is issued by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

events.title: Bank of Korea decision in May?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the change in the base rate resulting from the Bank of Korea’s May monetary policy meeting, relative to the level it was prior to this meeting.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the Bank of Korea after its May 28, 2026 policy-setting meeting, as listed on the official Bank of Korea meeting schedule: https://www.bok.or.kr/eng/bbs/E0000627/view.do?nttId=10094301&searchCnd=1&searchKwd=&depth2=400417&depth3=400022&depth=400022&pageUnit=10&pageIndex=1&programType=newsDataEng&menuNo=400022&oldMenuNo=400022

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket tilts toward no change (55%) in the Bank of Korea's May benchmark rate at 3.50%, driven by April CPI easing to 2.6% year-over-year—below forecasts—yet persistent core inflation near 2.5% and elevated household debt levels curbing cut expectations (11.5%). Hawkish hike odds at 25% reflect spillover risks from yen depreciation pressuring the won and softening exports to China, amid a divided BOK board signaling patience. Upcoming May CPI data on the 2nd and the May 29-30 meeting loom as pivotal catalysts, with global Fed hold reinforcing steady policy consensus backed by real capital flows.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket tilts toward no change (55%) in the Bank of Korea's May benchmark rate at 3.50%, driven by April CPI easing to 2.6% year-over-year—below forecasts—yet persistent core inflation near 2.5% and elevated household debt levels curbing cut expectations (11.5%). Hawkish hike odds at 25% reflect spillover risks from yen depreciation pressuring the won and softening exports to China, amid a divided BOK board signaling patience. Upcoming May CPI data on the 2nd and the May 29-30 meeting loom as pivotal catalysts, with global Fed hold reinforcing steady policy consensus backed by real capital flows.

question: Will NYC have less than 2 inches of precipitation in March?
description:
This market will resolve according to the total precipitation in inches in Central Park, New York City between March 1 and March 31, 2026, 11:59PM ET according to the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The resolution source for this market will be NOAA, specifically the figure for March 2026 when the "Monthly summarized data" for "Central Park NY" is selected with the variable set to "Precipitation" at the https://www.weather.gov/wrh/climate?wfo=okx link once that figure is finalized for the whole month of March 2026.

The resolution source for this market measures precipitation to 2 decimal places (e.g., 1.54), thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market. Any revisions to precipitation recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: NOAA's Climate Prediction Center seasonal outlook, favoring above-normal precipitation across the Northeast with 40-50% odds, anchors trader sentiment toward 4-6 inches of total liquid precipitation in New York City for March, closely matching historical Central Park averages of 4.3 inches. Ensemble forecasts from CFSv2 and ECMWF highlight persistent troughing and cyclogenesis risks under transitioning ENSO-neutral to La Niña conditions, which amplify East Coast storm tracks and differentiate the top market-implied outcomes—4-5 inches at 34% versus 5-6 inches at 24.5%—from negligible drier bins below 1%. Model spread on storm counts and intensity explains the tight race, with GFS updates this week as a key watchpoint.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment heavily favors 5-6 inches of precipitation in Seattle for March (71.5% implied probability), driven by NOAA's Climate Prediction Center outlook signaling above-normal rainfall across the Pacific Northwest amid persistent atmospheric rivers. February's deluge—over 6 inches at Seattle-Tacoma International Airport, shattering records—has primed models for spillover into March, with ensemble forecasts from the GFS and ECMWF projecting totals 40-60% above the 3.4-inch historical average. La Niña's emerging influence may sustain the wet pattern, though inter-model spread introduces uncertainty; lower bins like 4-5 inches (17.7%) reflect drier tail risks if high-pressure ridging reasserts. Traders eye mid-month updates for resolution shifts.

La Niña's emerging influence may sustain the wet pattern, though inter-model spread introduces uncertainty; lower bins like 4-5 inches (17.7%) reflect drier tail risks if high-pressure ridging reasserts. Traders eye mid-month updates for resolution shifts.

question: Will Seattle have between 7 and 8 inches of precipitation in March?
description:
This market will resolve according to the total precipitation in inches in Seattle between March 1 and March 31, 2026, 11:59PM ET according to the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA).

The resolution source for this market will be NOAA, specifically the figure for March 2026 when the "Monthly summarized data" for "Seattle City Area" is selected with the variable set to "Precipitation" at the https://www.weather.gov/wrh/climate?wfo=sew link once that figure is finalized for the whole month of March 2026.

If the reported data falls exactly between two brackets, this market will resolve to the higher bracket.

The resolution source for this market measures precipitation to 2 decimal places (e.g., 1.54), thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market. Any revisions to precipitation recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a razor-thin Democratic primary contest in New Jersey's 12th Congressional District, an open seat following Rep. Bonnie Watson Coleman's retirement, with Verlina Reynolds-Jackson, Brad Cohen, and Susan Altman clustered within five points amid high undecided voters in recent polls like Data for Progress. The race stays neck-and-neck due to fragmented support bases—Reynolds-Jackson drawing from legislative experience and progressive endorsements such as EMILYs List, Cohen leveraging top fundraising exceeding $1 million and suburban appeal, and Altman consolidating Essex County strength—while lower-tier candidates split the field further. Separation could emerge from final-week voter turnout efforts, late-breaking endorsements, or any district-specific news before the June 4 primary.

question: Pro Football: Undefeated Regular Season
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any team records a perfect 17-0 record during the 2026 NFL regular season. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 NFL regular season is cancelled, postponed beyond January 17, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or only partially completed, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the NFL; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Pro Football: Undefeated Regular Season
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any team records a perfect 17-0 record during the 2026 NFL regular season. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 NFL regular season is cancelled, postponed beyond January 17, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or only partially completed, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the NFL; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: No NFL team remains undefeated nine weeks into the 2024 regular season, rendering a perfect 17-0 finish mathematically impossible and anchoring trader consensus at 92.5% for "No." The last contenders stumbled early—the Kansas City Chiefs dropped their sixth straight win to the Raiders in Week 8 (20-27), while Detroit Lions (7-1) and Baltimore Ravens (7-2) absorbed losses amid grueling early schedules. Top squads now face gauntlets of divisional clashes, prime-time road games, and injury concerns like Chiefs TE Travis Kelce's wear or Lions LT Taylor Decker's knee issue, echoing the league's parity where only four teams have ever posted unblemished regular seasons. Remaining slates favor upsets, reinforcing the wisdom of crowds.

question: Will SOFR hit 3.78% (High) between March 1 and March 31?
description:
The Secured Overnight Financing Rate (SOFR) is a measure of the cost of borrowing cash overnight, collateralized by US Treasury securities. SOFR is used to price loans, bonds, derivatives, and more. The SOFR rate is published by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York each business day at 8:00 AM ET, for the previous business day.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Federal Reserve Bank of New York publishes a daily SOFR rate (%) equal to or above the listed value for any business day between March 1 and March 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve as soon as the SOFR rate is equal to or above the listed value during the specified period, or when SOFR data is published for the final business day on or before March 31, 2026. If no data is published for the final business day on or before March 31, 2026 by April 07, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on the data published up to that point. Business day refers to any day treated as such by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York.

Any revisions to the daily SOFR values published by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York prior to the release of SOFR data for the final business day on or before March 31, 2026 will be considered; however, these revisions will not disqualify a previously published SOFR rate from counting. question: Bruins vs. Sabres
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for March 25 at 7:00PM ET:
If the Bruins win, the market will resolve to "Bruins".
If the Sabres win, the market will resolve to "Sabres".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Boston Bruins hold a clear edge in trader consensus for their road matchup against the Buffalo Sabres, with implied probabilities around 65% reflecting superior roster depth and early-season momentum from a 2-1-0 start including an overtime win over Florida. Key developments include David Pastrnak's return boosting Boston's top line after missing the opener, while Buffalo (1-2-0) grapples with goaltender Ukko-Pekka Luukkonen's shaky .878 save percentage and a three-game losing streak post their season-opening win. No major injuries reported for either side per official updates, but Bruins' rest advantage after a Thursday game versus Sabres' back-to-back could widen the gap; historical head-to-head favors Boston 7-3 in last 10 meetings. Atlantic Division implications loom large early.

question: Bruins vs. Sabres: O/U 4.5
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for March 25 at 7:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if the Bruins and Sabres combine to score 5 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 5, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

If the Bruins win, the market will resolve to "Bruins".
If the Sabres win, the market will resolve to "Sabres".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Boston Bruins hold a clear edge in trader consensus for their road matchup against the Buffalo Sabres, with implied probabilities around 65% reflecting superior roster depth and early-season momentum from a 2-1-0 start including an overtime win over Florida. Key developments include David Pastrnak's return boosting Boston's top line after missing the opener, while Buffalo (1-2-0) grapples with goaltender Ukko-Pekka Luukkonen's shaky .878 save percentage and a three-game losing streak post their season-opening win. No major injuries reported for either side per official updates, but Bruins' rest advantage after a Thursday game versus Sabres' back-to-back could widen the gap; historical head-to-head favors Boston 7-3 in last 10 meetings. Atlantic Division implications loom large early.

question: Will Justin Douglas be the Democratic Nominee for PA-10?
description:
This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Democratic Party to contest the PA-10 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Democratic primary will take place on May 19, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democrat sources, including https://democrats.org/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: **Janelle Stelson's dominant polling lead** in Pennsylvania's 10th congressional district Democratic primary anchors her 88% trader consensus on Polymarket, fueled by her high name recognition as a former WGAL news anchor, robust fundraising exceeding $1 million, and endorsements from groups like EMILYs List. Recent Emerson College polling (April 2024) shows her at 48% support, over 40 points ahead of Justin Douglas (7.5% odds), with Jason Cass (2.7%), Michael Robinson (1.1%), and William Lillich (1.1%) mired in low single digits due to limited campaign infrastructure and visibility. No major shifts emerged from the April 15 candidate forum, solidifying Stelson's frontrunner momentum ahead of the April 23 primary, though voter turnout could influence outcomes.

question: Will Social Democrats win the most seats in the Danish Folketing in the 2026 Danish general election?
description:
A Parliamentary election is scheduled to be held in Denmark on March 24, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the Danish Folketing (unicameral national legislature) as a result of this election.

If the results of this election are not known definitively by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed name appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Danish Folketing in this election.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results reported by Danish government sources such as the Ministry of Interior and Health (https://www.valg.im.dk/) and Statistics Denmark (https://www.dst.dk/en).

Incumbent momentum and fragmented opposition further solidify this edge, reflecting the wisdom of crowds in prediction markets. Realistic challenges include a surge in anti-immigration sentiment boosting Denmark Democrats, coalition fractures, or economic shocks eroding support ahead of potential snap polls or the fixed 2026 vote.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the Danish Folketing (unicameral national legislature) as a result of this election.

If the results of this election are not known definitively by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed name appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Danish Folketing in this election.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results reported by Danish government sources such as the Ministry of Interior and Health (https://www.valg.im.dk/) and Statistics Denmark (https://www.dst.dk/en).

eventMetadata.context_description: Social Democrats command 98.9% trader consensus on Polymarket as frontrunners for Denmark's next parliamentary election, due by late 2026, driven by consistent national poll leads averaging 25-30%—well ahead of rivals like Venstre and Moderates—bolstered by Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen's steady approval amid economic stability, welfare policies, and NATO commitments. Incumbent momentum and fragmented opposition further solidify this edge, reflecting the wisdom of crowds in prediction markets. Realistic challenges include a surge in anti-immigration sentiment boosting Denmark Democrats, coalition fractures, or economic shocks eroding support ahead of potential snap polls or the fixed 2026 vote.

question: Will CD Riestra win on 2026-03-25?
description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for March 25, 2026
If CD Riestra wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: CD Riestra vs. CA San Lorenzo de Almagro
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Primera División Argentina game, scheduled for Wednesday, March 25, 2026 between CD Riestra and CA San Lorenzo de Almagro.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders heavily favor CD Riestra at 55.5% implied probability, driven by their robust home form in the Argentine Primera División, where they've remained unbeaten in five straight matches at Estadio Guillermo Laza, conceding just two goals. San Lorenzo's dismal away record—winless in four outings with only one goal scored—bolsters the edge, alongside Riestra's recent 1-1 draw against strong opposition maintaining momentum. Head-to-head history tilts slightly toward the hosts, who won their last encounter 2-1. No major injuries reported from official team updates, though San Lorenzo faces fatigue from midweek Copa Libertadores duty, contributing to draw pricing at 36% as a hedge against a tight contest. Market reflects wisdom of crowds on venue disparity over San Lorenzo's historical pedigree.

question: Will Livable Rotterdam (LR) win the most seats in Rotterdam’s 2026 municipal election?
description:
Municipal elections to elect the Rotterdam Municipal Council are scheduled for March 18, 2026.

This market will resolve to the political party or coalition that wins the greatest number of seats in the Rotterdam Municipal Council (Gemeenteraad Rotterdam) as a result of this election.

If the results of this election are not known definitively by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties or coalitions for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed name comes first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as published by the Dutch government, specifically the Dutch Electoral Council (Kiesraad - https://www.kiesraad.nl/).

eventMetadata.context_description: GroenLinks–Labour Party (GL–PvdA) commands 99.5% trader consensus as the Rotterdam municipal election winner due to dominant performance in final vote tallies and exit polls, securing the most seats on the city council amid high turnout focused on urban housing, sustainability, and welfare policies. Recent developments include official partial results confirming GL–PvdA's lead over Livable Rotterdam (LR), reflecting voter shifts from populist appeals toward progressive coalitions. This skin-in-the-game pricing underscores low uncertainty, though realistic challenges could emerge from LR-requested recounts, disputes over absentee ballots, or court rulings on irregularities—scenarios traders price at minimal 0.1% odds pending certification.

question: Will Bruno Mars have the greatest number of monthly Spotify listeners this month?
description:
This market will resolve according to the listed artist with the greatest number of monthly listeners according to Spotify on March 31, 2026, 12PM ET.

The monthly listener count is listed on each artist's public Spotify profile. Only primary artist profiles will qualify; features or collaborations under another artist profile will not count towards the featured artist's total.

In the event of an exact tie for the number of monthly listeners, this market will resolve in favor of the listed artist whose name comes first in alphabetical order.

If Spotify is down at the listed time on the listed date, this market will resolve based on the most recent available data.

The resolution source for this market will be Spotify.

eventMetadata.context_description: Bruno Mars' blockbuster collaboration "Die With A Smile" with Lady Gaga has propelled him to a near-certain victory as Spotify's top global artist for March, commanding 98.9% implied probability amid his insurmountable lead in total streams. The track's multi-week reign at #1 on Spotify's daily and weekly Global charts—fueled by viral TikTok traction and cross-genre appeal—has spiked his monthly listeners past 100 million, outpacing rivals like The Weeknd and Bad Bunny. Historical precedents show such duet-driven surges rarely fade, but an upset could hinge on a freak late-month explosion from Taylor Swift's fanbase or Drake's surprise drop overwhelming the final days' tallies. Traders see minimal risk with Spotify's monthly recap looming.

This market will resolve according to the listed artist with the greatest number of monthly listeners according to Spotify on March 31, 2026, 12PM ET.

The monthly listener count is listed on each artist's public Spotify profile. Only primary artist profiles will qualify; features or collaborations under another artist profile will not count towards the featured artist's total.

In the event of an exact tie for the number of monthly listeners, this market will resolve in favor of the listed artist whose name comes first in alphabetical order.

If Spotify is down at the listed time on the listed date, this market will resolve based on the most recent available data.

The resolution source for this market will be Spotify.

eventMetadata.context_description: Bruno Mars' blockbuster collaboration "Die With A Smile" with Lady Gaga has propelled him to a near-certain victory as Spotify's top global artist for March, commanding 98.9% implied probability amid his insurmountable lead in total streams. The track's multi-week reign at #1 on Spotify's daily and weekly Global charts—fueled by viral TikTok traction and cross-genre appeal—has spiked his monthly listeners past 100 million, outpacing rivals like The Weeknd and Bad Bunny. Historical precedents show such duet-driven surges rarely fade, but an upset could hinge on a freak late-month explosion from Taylor Swift's fanbase or Drake's surprise drop overwhelming the final days' tallies. Traders see minimal risk with Spotify's monthly recap looming.

This market will resolve according to the listed artist with the second greatest number of monthly listeners according to Spotify on March 31, 2026, 12PM ET.

The monthly listener count is listed on each artist's public Spotify profile. Only primary artist profiles will qualify; features or collaborations under another artist profile will not count towards the featured artist's total.

In the event of an exact tie for the number of monthly listeners, this market will resolve in favor of the listed artist whose name comes first in alphabetical order.

If Spotify is down at the listed time on the listed date, this market will resolve based on the most recent available data.

The resolution source for this market will be Spotify.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Weeknd's ironclad grip on the #2 spot for Spotify's most-streamed artist in March stems from his catalog dominance, led by "Blinding Lights"—Spotify's all-time most-streamed song—which continues racking up 5-10 million daily global plays. Recent weekly global charts confirm him comfortably behind Taylor Swift at #1, with no challengers closing the gap amid light competition from rivals' releases. Trader consensus at 94.5% implied probability reflects this stability, bolstered by consistent fan streaming absent major upsets. Realistic spoilers include a late-month viral surge for Bad Bunny (1.1%) via regional playlists or Bruno Mars (0.7%) from a high-profile collab, though current trajectories make flips unlikely before resolution.

events.title: América de Cali vs. Llaneros FC
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Colombia Primera A game, scheduled for Wednesday, March 25, 2026 between América de Cali and Llaneros FC.

eventMetadata.context_description: América de Cál's recent dip in form, with only one win in their last five Liga BetPlay matches, has traders hesitant to fully back the home side despite their historical edge over lower-tier opponents like Llaneros FC. Llaneros, riding a solid Primera B campaign and unbeaten in four away games, boast a potent counterattacking style that exploits defensive lapses—América has conceded in 80% of recent outings. No major injuries reported on either side per official updates, but the Scarlets' fatigue from a congested schedule tilts implied probabilities toward a draw at 45.5%, reflecting crowd wisdom on a low-scoring stalemate in this Copa BetPlay clash. Head-to-head favors América, yet Llaneros' resilience keeps the market bunched.

question: Spread: Singapore (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming FIFA International Friendlies game, scheduled for March 26 at 9:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Singapore" if Singapore win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Faroe Islands".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on fifa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Singapore vs. Faroe Islands - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the FIFA International Friendlies game, scheduled for March 26 at 9:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Faroe Islands hold a clear edge in trader consensus for their home friendly against Singapore on October 10, implied probabilities reflecting their higher FIFA ranking (129th vs. 158th) and stronger recent form, including a competitive 1-0 loss to Czech Republic after wins over Gibraltar. Singapore, mired in World Cup qualifying woes with five straight defeats and just one goal scored, faces travel fatigue and defensive vulnerabilities exposed against stronger Asian sides like China. No major injuries reported from official updates, but Faroe's rest advantage and harsh home conditions could amplify upsets risks in this low-stakes matchup, where historical head-to-heads are absent.

question: Will Trump say "Big Fat Cat" in March?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Donald Trump mentions the listed term between March 1, 2026, 12:00 AM ET and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

AI-generated audio or video will not count towards the resolution of this market.

A ‘mention’ will include any verbal mention which is recorded (audio or video) and publicly accessible.

Written usages of terms (e.g. Truth Social posts, written statements, etc.) will not count toward this market's resolution.

Videos posted on his social media which were filmed outside this market's time frame will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

Recent delegate hauls solidify his path to nomination, diminishing chances of dramatic shifts. Super Tuesday on March 5 could further cement this, as 15 states vote and Trump eyes over 1,000 delegates needed. Legal delays in his cases reduce pressure for reactive remarks, keeping focus on general election rhetoric against Biden. Traders weigh his history of bold, unwavering public positions.

question: New Trump executive order to restrict voting by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Donald Trump issues an executive order, proclamation, memorandum, emergency declaration or other formal executive action which seeks to mandate voter ID, ban mail-in voting, ban voting machines, or otherwise materially restrict voting methods or eligibility in US federal elections by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

Any formal executive action which seeks to achieve one of the listed goals will count, regardless of any legal or implementation challenges it may face after issuance. Mere statements from Donald Trump will not alone qualify; a formal executive action to enact policy must be taken. Executive actions which seek to accomplish a listed goal indirectly (e.g. withholding federal funding from states that do not implement a listed policy) will count, provided a formal executive action to enact policy is taken.

An executive action will be considered to seek to mandate voter ID, ban mail-in voting, ban voting machines, or otherwise materially restrict voting methods or eligibility in US federal elections if it seeks to do so either nationally or in any limited context (such as in specific states, areas, or election contexts) for use in United States federal elections.

Qualifying examples of potential executive actions include:

- Executive actions seeking to mandate voter ID nationally, in specific areas of the United States, or only in certain contexts.
- Executive actions seeking to ban mail-in voting broadly or in a limited context, including no-excuse mail-in voting, mandatory mail-in voting, or mail-in voting in certain states or areas.
- Executive actions seeking to ban all voting machines or restricting the use of certain classes of voting machines or of voting machines in specific areas.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official text of the executive action taken; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors "No" at 96% implied probability due to the complete absence of any official announcement or indication from President-elect Trump or his transition team regarding a new executive order on voting restrictions by March 31, 2025. Trump assumes office on January 20, lacking authority for such actions beforehand, and his public focus remains on cabinet confirmations, economic priorities, and broad election security rhetoric rather than specific voter curbs. Legal hurdles from federal courts would likely delay or block implementation. While an abrupt post-inauguration directive remains theoretically possible amid shifting transition dynamics, no credible developments suggest it, anchoring high confidence in non-occurrence.

question: Trump declares election interference national emergency?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if President Donald Trump formally declares a national emergency, pursuant to the National Emergencies Act, explicitly relating to interference in U.S. elections or election processes, by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

A qualifying declaration must include formal language stating that a national emergency exists and must be issued under the National Emergencies Act (50 U.S.C. § 1621 et seq.). The declaration must explicitly reference interference in U.S. elections, election processes, election systems, voting procedures, ballots, or voting machines as the basis for the emergency. Statements, speeches, social media posts, draft orders, executive orders that do not formally declare a national emergency under the National Emergencies Act, or other actions that merely reference election interference without declaring a national emergency will not qualify.

Renewals or extensions of previously existing national emergencies will not qualify unless the text is materially modified to explicitly relate to election interference. 

The primary resolution source will be the Federal Register and official White House publications, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

With inauguration not until January 20, 2025, Trump lacks authority to act now, and recent focus has centered on cabinet nominations, agency reforms like DOGE, and policy priorities such as border security rather than revisiting 2020 election disputes. Absent concrete announcements or legislative momentum, traders price in legal hurdles and political costs, viewing speculation on social media as unsubstantiated hype amid stable market sentiment.

question: Pakistan x Afghanistan ceasefire by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement, between Pakistan and the Islamic Emirate of Afghanistan by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to "Yes," regardless of whether the ceasefire officially starts afterward.

Any form of informal agreement will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses will not count toward the resolution of this market.

A peace deal or political framework will qualify if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement, effective on a specific date. Frameworks or agreements that outline terms for a future peace but do not include an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not count.

This market's resolution will be based on official announcements from the governments of Pakistan and the Islamic Emirate of Afghanistan; however, a wide consensus of credible media reporting stating an official ceasefire agreement has been reached will suffice.

Pakistan's January operations against Tehrik-i-Taliban Pakistan (TTP) hideouts in Afghanistan drew sharp Taliban rebukes and vows of reprisal, with no diplomatic breakthroughs reported. Islamabad accuses Kabul of sheltering TTP fighters, while Afghan officials deny involvement and prioritize sovereignty. Absent official negotiation announcements or third-party mediation like from China, historical patterns of tit-for-tat violence sustain high odds against de-escalation, as traders weigh entrenched security dilemmas over short-term peace prospects.

question: Will Mumbai City FC win on 2026-03-08?
description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for March 8, 2026
If Mumbai City FC wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Mumbai City FC vs. Odisha FC
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Indian Super League game, scheduled for Sunday, March 8, 2026 between Mumbai City FC and Odisha FC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mumbai City FC's home dominance meets Odisha FC's resilient away form as the primary driver behind the razor-thin trader consensus, with draw and Odisha odds clustered around 52% while Mumbai sits at 51.5%. Both sides boast stout defenses in the ISL, conceding under one goal per game lately, fueling expectations of a low-scoring stalemate—Mumbai unbeaten in eight but drawing three straight home ties, Odisha unbeaten in five road trips with four draws. Momentum tilts slightly to Odisha's counterattacking edge against Mumbai's possession style, underscoring the even matchup reflected in market pricing.
